titleStmt.title: Snake Killing Harran
SHAWI Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the FWF Austrian Science Fund under the number P 33574

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the SHAWI teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the SHAWI network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the dict prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a entry in the SHAWI glossary.
Private URIs using the rend prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a rendition element in VICAV rendition library.
Private URIs using the assets prefix are pointers to the location of the SHAWI media assets.

spanGrp.span: Uncle Khalil, how did you kill the snake?
We would catch it and kill it.
Of course! Ismail says that you didn’t dare to kill it.
You didn’t dare to kill it at first.
No, later I dared.
When it appeared I took a rod, three or four meters long.
A stick.
Yes, and I was grasping it and killed it.
With the stick.
With the stick.
ha? We buried it.
Tell us how? Did you see it?
I saw the birds making noise.
Yes.
So, I thought to myself, “Let me see what’s there!” I was sitting there and looked at it; and it turned out to be a snake and then I killed it!
Did you scream at it and said to it, “Hoosh!"
Yes.
What did you say to it?
I said, “Hoosh!"
Stop!
Yes, if I had not killed it...
At the wall I said to it, “Hoosh!” I screamed at it, “Hoosh!” It was trying to climb up.
This (story) was recently; let me tell you that it was my child who found it. He found it in our room; we have a room that is unoccupied there.
And he said, “Dad, there’s a snake!” And it came closer to us.
So, it means, everyone was at home at that time?
Yes, everyone was at home.
Last year we were sleeping in the living room. My daughter wanted to sweep the floor and suddenly a snake of that size appeared to her!
I was sleeping under the blanket like this, and I ran away, I ran out into the open. I ran away from it, and we didn’t kill it. Maybe it was the same snake.
Maybe they have something, but they remain dangerous. You can’t trust a snake.
Yes, it’s never certain.
Not all of them are poisonous.
No, not all of them are poisonous.
No, they’re not poisonous.
There are also non-poisonous ones.
Yes, yes.
But the poisonous one was killed!
Of course,
this snake wasn’t terrestrial.
It was a water snake.
A water snake, its colour was green, it was of the green type, but it was black, black.
Yes, completely black.
Yes, black.
As if it was painted black.
They should be red, but the ones here are multicoloured mixed with red.
No, they’re black.
Originally, we have many here
Like black shoe polish.
The water brings them here.
Yes, they come with the water.
Aren’t our rooms made of stone?
Yes!
They go and hide inside the stones. We’ve found several of them here.
They hide between the stones
Yes.
I called out to Fatima and said, “Fatima, get up, it’s a snake!” She ran away!
And she said, “Xalīl al-Ḥəlu kills snakes.” So, they called on him to kill the snake.
Yes, it was truly like this.
God gave me a long stick, and by God, it didn’t approach it.
Yes, in our rooms there are many, not just a few.
There are really many.
Yes, not a few, many.
The water brings them.
They are in crevices, therein they are.
They are in uninhabited areas.
The world
Ismail, if you had seen it, you would have said, “This is (just) a black cord.” The thickness of its body was like this. You surely would have said, “I want to grasp it, it’s a cord.”
Its length was over a meter and a half.
Yes, over a meter and a half.
A meter and a half, it was a meter and a half.
When the weather is hot it’s dangerous; in the summer.
Yes, in the summer. If it were in the summer, it wouldn’t have been caught.
It cannot be caught in the summer because it offers resistance. (This time) the weather was normal, it wasn’t considered summer.
Ismail, once we had a pit here for the auger and I wanted to take a motor pump. At that time, it was about 3 metres. Like this, three metres (deep).
And I was about to climb down in the tube in order to take air for the pump. By God, suddenly it is at my hand. One which they call kobān.
He understands, he understands, he knows. It came to my hand and then I grabbed it with my hand like this.
As soon as I had grabbed it with my hand, Muḥammad came out (from the pit) and this without a ladder!
And then Ismail came and killed it.
Does Ismail have the ability?
Yes.
Ismail isn’t afraid of them (the snakes).
Yes.
Ismail isn’t afraid of it, and Ahmed isn’t afraid of it either.
Yes.
Ahmed also isn’t afraid of it.
He is struggling with it while holding it in his hand.
He grabs the snake with his hand before killing it, he holds it in his hand. Ahmed isn’t afraid of it.
Once I was riding my motorbike and suddenly it appeared in front of me on the road. I thought, even if I run over it, nothing will happen to it.
Nothing will happen to it.
I stood there waiting for it to move away.
You didn’t bother it?
I didn’t have a stick or a stone with me.
And you weren’t scared?
No. And then (the snake) disappeared.
There are many of them here; the water brings them.
Yes, the water brings them.
The water, the water brings them.
You can find many of them in the irrigation channels and in water troughs made of concrete.
Many.
A lot of them. They all come from the water reservoirs, from the mountains.
Yes.
The water from the reservoir brings them.
